title: Memory Reactivation Levels Remain Unaffected by Anticipated Interference Experiment 2 Dataset
description: This dataset contains EEG recordings from Experiment 2 of a study investigating whether memory reactivation levels are affected by anticipated interference during a dual-task paradigm. Participants performed a memory task with varying cognitive load conditions (single task with fixation vs. dual task with distractors), while neural activity was recorded to examine memory maintenance mechanisms under interference. Each trial began with a space key press, followed by a fixation dot (500–650 ms), then 2 or 6 lateral objects appeared for 1250 ms with a central cue indicating which object(s) to memorize. After a 1000 ms delay, participants either performed a dual task (identifying a same-category distractor object among 4 distractors) or single task (fixation only). Memory was tested with a two-alternative forced choice probe using arrow keys. Preprocessing details are available in the associated OSF repository.
methods_description: Each trial consisted of a space key press initiation, followed by a fixation dot presentation (500–650 ms duration). Memory encoding phase presented 2 or 6 lateral objects for 1250 ms, with a central cue indicating which object(s) to memorize. The contralateral side displayed irrelevant objects for visual balance. After a 1000 ms delay period, participants performed either a dual task (identifying a same-category distractor object among 4 distractors with accuracy feedback via colored dot) or single task (fixation only). Memory was tested with a two-alternative forced choice probe, where participants selected the cued object using arrow keys, followed by feedback showing the correct object with colored cues. EEG data were preprocessed using standard procedures as detailed in the associated OSF repository.
